, April 6
Your day was focused on a few parallel threads. You confirmed a staged prod deploy was working in #apollos-customer-success, and you set up some integrations in the #graphite-webhook channel (Instatus). You also spent a good chunk of the morning helping Vitor debug a tap tag/range fix on staging — guiding him through adjusting a record from 1 to 0 and reassuring him that the leading zero feedback was out of scope. In parallel, you connected with Michael Neeley about the Stripe Gross Settlement project, floating a time to sync later in the week.

, April 7
A busier product-focused day. You kicked off two threads in #product: one around rebuilding the Admin Payouts table (the current one is a Stripe component, so hiding fees requires a full rebuild — you decided to just match the Recurring Gifts table without waiting on designs), and another about the Transaction Row Details modal, working through what data to show (Succeeded/Refunded statuses, the ConnectPaymentDetails component) and whether to keep the Begin Refund button.
You also started a thread in #dev about improving the PR review process — proposing that everyone have a primary reviewer paired with them rather than the "anyone can review" pool, which was feeling like asking favors.
In the afternoon you dove into an OpenAI spend spike investigation, pulling logs and tracing it to what looked like a single user (a church content admin) going on a generation spree in AI Insights. You concluded it wasn't a systemic loop and said you'd keep monitoring. You also noted you'll be OOO Thursday and Friday.

, April 8 (today)
This morning was largely engineering coordination. You advised on using a LaunchDarkly flag flip rather than removing code to lock down a feature quickly. You got into a thread about AWS preview app URL configuration — specifically getting REACT_APP_BASE_URL set per-app to handle staging vs. prod environments (referencing the old Heroku approach). There was also some debugging around email invite flows in preview apps.
You flagged a likely bug with Pushpay staging URLs (it was pushing to prod last week, now needs a staging URL), and asked someone to file a ticket. The OpenAI spend also re-upped this morning but usage looked normal.
This afternoon you kicked off a thread with Dylan on Universal Giving Export after watching his Loom.
